title: Nations Have the Right to Kill
note: Restricted metadata only until source access is clarified.

title: Proclamation to the German Nation
note: Radio broadcast, 1 February 1933. Reproduced in Baynes, Norman H., ed., The Speeches of Adolf Hitler, April 1922–August 1939, Vol. 1 (London: Oxford University Press, 1942). Passage text requires verification against Baynes or equivalent scholarly edition before advancing beyond source-review.

title: War Powers Resolution
note: Public Law 93-148; 87 Stat. 555; codified at 50 U.S.C. §§ 1541–1548. Public domain US federal law.

title: Address to the Nation on Energy and National Goals ("Crisis of Confidence")
note: Public domain US government document. Archived at the Jimmy Carter Presidential Library and the Miller Center, University of Virginia.

title: Resignation Address
note: Broadcast on Soviet state television, 25 December 1991. English translation reproduced in Gorbachev, Mikhail, Memoirs (New York: Doubleday, 1996) and widely in press archives. Passage text requires verification against a scholarly edition.

title: Alma-Ata Declaration

author.literal: Commonwealth of Independent States founding states

note: Signed 21 December 1991 by eleven former Soviet republics. Public domain. Reproduced in International Legal Materials 31:1 (1992), p. 148.

title: Imperial Rescript on Education (Kyōiku Chokugo)
note: Public domain. English translation published by the Japanese Ministry of Education. Widely reproduced in Tsunoda, Ryusaku, Wm. Theodore de Bary, and Donald Keene, eds., Sources of Japanese Tradition, Vol. 2 (New York: Columbia University Press, 1958).

title: Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors (Gunjin Chokuyu)
note: Public domain. English translation in McLaren, W. W., ed., Japanese Government Documents, Transactions of the Asiatic Society of Japan 42 (1914).

title: Regierungserklärung (First Government Declaration)
note: Delivered to the Bundestag, 20 September 1949. Public domain. German text in Verhandlungen des Deutschen Bundestages, 1. Wahlperiode, 5. Sitzung. English translation in Adenauer, Konrad, Memoirs 1945–53 (London: Weidenfeld & Nicolson, 1966). Passage text requires verification against cited edition.

title: Basic Law for the Federal Republic of Germany (Grundgesetz)
note: Public domain. Official English translation published by the Deutscher Bundestag (2012 ed.). Art. 1(1): human dignity inviolable. Art. 79(3): eternity clause placing Art. 1 beyond amendment.

title: Program of the National Socialist German Workers' Party (25 Points)
note: Public domain. Announced 24 February 1920, Hofbräuhaus, Munich. Reproduced in Stackelberg, Roderick, and Sally A. Winkle, eds., The Nazi Germany Sourcebook (London: Routledge, 2002), pp. 27–31.

title: Constitution of Democratic Kampuchea
note: Adopted 5 January 1976. Available at https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Constitution_of_Democratic_Kampuchea and DC-Cam (Documentation Center of Cambodia).

title: Dap Prampi Mesa Chokchey (National Anthem of Democratic Kampuchea)
note: National anthem of Democratic Kampuchea (1975–1979). English translation available at https://nationalanthems.info/kh-79.htm

title: Serve the People
note: Speech delivered at memorial service for Zhang Side, 8 September 1944. In Selected Works of Mao Tse-tung, Vol. 3 (Peking: Foreign Languages Press, 1965). Available at https://www.marxists.org/reference/archive/mao/selected-works/volume-3/mswv3_19.htm

title: In Memory of Norman Bethune
note: In Selected Works of Mao Tse-tung, Vol. 2 (Peking: Foreign Languages Press, 1965), pp. 337–338. Available at https://www.marxists.org/reference/archive/mao/selected-works/volume-2/mswv2_25.htm

title: Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen
note: Adopted by the National Constituent Assembly, 26 August 1789. Public domain; widely available in English translation. See https://avalon.law.yale.edu/18th_century/rightsof.asp

title: On the Principles of Political Morality That Should Guide the National Convention
note: Delivered to the National Convention, 5 February 1794 (18 Pluviôse, Year II). English translation available at https://revolution.chnm.org/d/413 and https://alphahistory.com/frenchrevolution/robespierre-virtue-terror-1794/

title: Address to the Reichstag on the Outbreak of War
note: Throne speech delivered at the opening of the Reichstag, 4 August 1914. English translation in German History in Documents and Images (GHDI): https://ghdi.ghi-dc.org/sub_document.cfm?document_id=815

title: Message to the Chambers on the State of War with Germany
note: Read to the French Chambers by the Minister of Justice, 4 August 1914. English translation at https://www.firstworldwar.com/source/poincare_aug1914.htm and https://wwi.lib.byu.edu/index.php/President_Poincar%C3%A9s_War_Message

title: Constitution of the Confederate States of America
note: Adopted 11 March 1861. Public domain. Full text at Avalon Project, Yale Law School: https://avalon.law.yale.edu/19th_century/csa_csa.asp

title: Cornerstone Speech
note: Delivered at the Athenaeum, Savannah, Georgia, 21 March 1861. Transcribed in Savannah Republican; public domain. Available at https://www.battlefields.org/learn/primary-sources/cornerstone-speech and https://teachingamericanhistory.org/document/the-corner-stone-speech/

title: De Re Militari (Epitoma Rei Militaris)
note: Written c. 390 AD. Public domain. English translation by Lt. John Clarke (1767) available at Internet Archive: https://archive.org/details/bim_eighteenth-century_de-re-militari-english_vegetius-renatus-flaviu_1767

title: City of God (De Civitate Dei)
note: Written 413–426 AD. Public domain. Marcus Dods English translation available at New Advent: https://www.newadvent.org/fathers/1201.htm

title: Gettysburg Address
note: Delivered at Soldiers' National Cemetery, Gettysburg PA, 19 November 1863. Public domain. Bliss copy (definitive version) text available at abrahamlincolnonline.org and ushistory.org/documents/gettysburg.htm

title: Second Inaugural Address
note: Delivered 4 March 1865. Public domain. Verified at Avalon Project, Yale Law School: https://avalon.law.yale.edu/19th_century/lincoln2.asp

title: Authorization for Use of Military Force
note: Public Law 107-40, 115 Stat. 224, signed 18 September 2001. Full text at govinfo.gov: https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/PLAW-107publ40/html/PLAW-107publ40.htm

title: Address to a Joint Session of Congress and the American People
note: Delivered to Joint Session of Congress, 20 September 2001. Public domain. Text verified at https://www.whatsoproudlywehail.org/curriculum/the-american-calendar/address-to-a-joint-session-of-congress-following-the-911-attacks/

title: Manifesto of the Communist Party
note: Published February 1848. Public domain. Samuel Moore English translation (1888) verified at https://www.marxists.org/archive/marx/works/1848/communist-manifesto/

title: Constitution (Fundamental Law) of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
note: Adopted 7 October 1977. English text verified at Bucknell University Russian Department: https://www.departments.bucknell.edu/russian/const/77cons01.html and Marxists.org: https://www.marxists.org/history/ussr/government/constitution/1977/

title: Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms
note: Schedule B to the Constitution Act, 1982, Canada. Enacted 17 April 1982. Official text at https://laws-lois.justice.gc.ca/eng/const/page-12.html

title: Treaty on European Union (Consolidated Version)
note: Signed Maastricht, 7 February 1992; consolidated version (2016) at EUR-Lex: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:12016ME/TXT. Official Journal of the EU, C 202, 7 June 2016.

title: Hind Swaraj, or Indian Home Rule
note: First published in Gujarati in Indian Opinion, 1909; English translation by the author, 1910. Public domain. Full text at https://www.gandhiashramsevagram.org/hind-swaraj/

title: Federal Constitution of the Swiss Confederation
note: Adopted by popular vote 18 April 1999, in force 1 January 2000. Official English translation at https://www.constituteproject.org/constitution/Switzerland_2014

title: Socialist Constitution of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea
note: Adopted 1972; revised 1992, 1998, 2009, 2012, 2016, 2019. English translation (2019 version) from University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa Korean Studies Center: https://manoa.hawaii.edu/koreanstudies/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/DPRK-Constitution-2019-EN.pdf. Access caveat: authoritative Korean-language text is on DPRK state website (Naenara); English translation accuracy is unverifiable without Korean-language expertise; University of Hawaii translation is the scholarly standard.

title: Inter-Imperial Relations Committee Report (Balfour Declaration)
note: Approved unanimously by the 1926 Imperial Conference of British Empire prime ministers, 15 November 1926. Chaired by Arthur Balfour. Text available at Founding Docs of Australia (https://www.foundingdocs.gov.au/item-did-24.html) and The Canadian Encyclopedia (https://www.thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/document-balfour-report-1926).

title: Statute of Westminster 1931
note: 22 Geo. 5, c. 4; Royal Assent 11 December 1931. Full text at legislation.gov.uk: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/Geo5/22-23/4/introduction

title: Oath of Allegiance of the Armed Forces to Adolf Hitler (2 August 1934)
note: Decreed 20 August 1934 as Law on the Allegiance of Civil Servants and Soldiers; sworn by the Reichswehr on 2 August 1934 following Hindenburg's death. English text verified at German History in Documents and Images (GHDI): https://germanhistorydocs.org/en/nazi-germany-1933-1945/the-reichswehr-swears-an-oath-of-allegiance-to-adolf-hitler-on-the-day-of-hindenburgs-death-august-2-1934

title: Law Against the Founding of New Parties (Gesetz gegen die Neubildung von Parteien)
note: Signed 14 July 1933 by Hitler, Frick, and Gürtner. English text verified at United States Holocaust Memorial Museum Encyclopedia: https://encyclopedia.ushmm.org/content/en/article/law-against-the-founding-of-new-parties

title: Kokutai no Hongi (Cardinal Principles of the National Polity of Japan)
note: Published by Japanese Ministry of Education, March 1937; 2+ million copies distributed. English translation in de Bary, Wm. Theodore, Carol Gluck, and Arthur L. Tiedemann, eds., Sources of Japanese Tradition, 2nd ed., vol. 2 (New York: Columbia University Press, 2005), pp. 968–975. Excerpt at Asia for Educators, Columbia University: https://afe.easia.columbia.edu/ps/japan/kokutai.pdf

title: Imperial Rescript Declaring War on the United States and the British Empire
note: Issued 8 December 1941 (Japan time). Public domain. English text verified at The World and Japan Database (worldjpn.net): https://worldjpn.net/documents/texts/docs/19411208.O1E.html

title: Report to the Plenary Session of the CPSU Central Committee
note: Delivered 27 January 1987; published in Pravda and Izvestiia, 28 January 1987. Excerpt verified at Seventeen Moments in Soviet History (Michigan State University): https://soviethistory.msu.edu/1985/perestroika-and-glasnost/perestroika-and-glasnost-texts/gorbachev-challenges-the-party/

title: Appeal to the Soviet People (GKChP)
note: Statement of the State Committee on the State of Emergency (GKChP), broadcast on Soviet radio and television starting 08:00, 19 August 1991. Passage verified at GlobalSecurity.org: https://www.globalsecurity.org/military/world/war/russia2.htm

title: The Uses of Military Power
note: Address to the National Press Club, Washington, D.C., 28 November 1984. Primary source: Office of the Historian, FRUS 1981–1988, vol. I, doc. 219 (https://history.state.gov/historicaldocuments/frus1981-88v01/d219); archived at PBS FRONTLINE (https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/pages/frontline/shows/military/force/weinberger.html).

title: Address Before the Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield, Illinois
note: Public domain. Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln vol.1 pp.108-115 (Basler ed.).

title: Speech at Peoria, Illinois
note: Public domain. Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln vol.2 pp.247-283 (Basler ed.).

title: House Divided Speech
note: Public domain. Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln vol.2 pp.461-469 (Basler ed.). Delivered as Republican State Convention speech accepting the senatorial nomination.

title: Address at Cooper Institute, New York City
note: Public domain. Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln vol.3 pp.522-550 (Basler ed.).

title: First Inaugural Address
note: Public domain. Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln vol.4 pp.262-271 (Basler ed.). Some passages revised by William H. Seward; authorshipConfidence: partial for affected passages.

title: Temperance Address
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.1 pp.271-279 (Basler ed.).

title: Eulogy on Henry Clay
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.2 pp.121-132 (Basler ed.).

title: First Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Ottawa, Illinois (Lincoln's portions)
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.3 pp.1-37 (Basler ed.).

title: Second Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Freeport, Illinois (Lincoln's portions)
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.3 pp.38-76 (Basler ed.).

title: Third Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Jonesboro, Illinois (Lincoln's portions)
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.3 pp.102-144 (Basler ed.).

title: Fourth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Charleston, Illinois (Lincoln's portions)
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.3 pp.145-201 (Basler ed.).

title: Fifth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Galesburg, Illinois (Lincoln's portions)
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.3 pp.207-244 (Basler ed.).

title: Sixth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Quincy, Illinois (Lincoln's portions)
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.3 pp.245-282 (Basler ed.).

title: Seventh Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Alton, Illinois (Lincoln's portions)
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.3 pp.283-325 (Basler ed.).

title: Farewell Address at Springfield, Illinois
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.4 pp.190-191 (Basler ed.).

title: Message to Congress in Special Session, July 4, 1861
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.4 pp.421-441 (Basler ed.).

title: Fragment on the Constitution and the Union
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.4 pp.168-169 (Basler ed.). Date uncertain; assigned January 1861 by Basler.

title: Letter to Horace Greeley
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.5 pp.388-389 (Basler ed.).

title: Preliminary Emancipation Proclamation
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.5 pp.433-436 (Basler ed.).

title: Annual Message to Congress, December 1, 1862
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.5 pp.518-537 (Basler ed.).

title: Emancipation Proclamation
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.6 pp.28-31 (Basler ed.). Final Proclamation.

title: Letter to James C. Conkling
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.6 pp.406-410 (Basler ed.).

title: Memorandum Concerning His Probable Failure of Re-election
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.7 pp.514-515 (Basler ed.). Blind memorandum.

title: Letter to Albert G. Hodges
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.7 pp.281-283 (Basler ed.).

title: Response to a Serenade, November 10, 1864
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.8 pp.96-97 (Basler ed.).

title: Last Public Address
note: Public domain. Collected Works vol.8 pp.399-405 (Basler ed.).
